name: French Way Alternative Route Loop
name_original: Símbolos del Camino de Santiago Francés en Tierra de Campos — Circular a orillas del río Ucieza
description: Hike a moderate 7.0-mile (11.3 km) loop along the Ucieza River, exploring Camino de Santiago symbols in Tierra de Campos.
region_intro: Hiking around Población De Campos offers routes through the flat, agricultural landscape of the Meseta, characterized by open fields and the historic Canal de Castilla. The region features gentle terrain with minimal elevation changes, making it accessible for various activity levels. Trails often follow the banks of the Canal de Castilla, providing views of its locks and surrounding rural areas. This area is ideal for those seeking tranquil walks through historical and natural settings. Best hiking trails around Población De Campos The most popular hiking route is Boadilla del Camino from Frómista — circular by the Canal de Castilla, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the historic Canal de Castilla. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fromista and its locks of the Canal de Castilla, an easy 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This trail explores the Canal de Castilla and its notable locks. Local hikers also love the Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through the flat, open landscape along the Canal, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Hiking around Población De Campos is defined by flat terrain, the historic Canal de Castilla, and open agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate routes. The routes in Población De Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Población De Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Población De Campos?
A: The hiking trails around Población De Campos are primarily characterized by the flat, agricultural landscape of the Meseta. You'll find open fields and routes often following the historic Canal de Castilla, with minimal elevation changes. This makes the area suitable for tranquil walks.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Población De Campos?
A: Yes, Población De Campos offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067302634/fromista-and-its-locks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista and its locks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.9 km) path exploring the Canal's notable locks. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.2-mile (5.2 km) trail through flat, open landscape.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in the area?
A: Many trails follow the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of its engineering marvels. You can specifically see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks&lt;/a&gt;. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant point of interest along some routes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available around Población De Campos?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104207227/boadilla-del-camino-from-fromista-circular-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino from Frómista — circular by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.9 miles (12.7 km) long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 3.2-mile (5.2 km) trail.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Población De Campos?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route. Easy paths like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Longer, moderate routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104207227/boadilla-del-camino-from-fromista-circular-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino from Frómista — circular by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours 14 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Población De Campos?
A: The trails in Población De Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil nature of the walks along the Canal de Castilla and the flat, accessible terrain.

Q: Is Población De Campos a good destination for winter hiking?
A: While the terrain is generally flat, winter in the Meseta can bring cold temperatures. Trails remain accessible, but it's advisable to check local weather forecasts for conditions like frost or occasional light snow. Dress in layers and wear appropriate footwear.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking routes?
A: The primary scenic spots are along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views of the waterway, its historic locks, and the expansive agricultural landscape of the Meseta. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/854237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Section of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its beauty.

Q: What should I bring for a hike in Población De Campos?
A: Given the open and often sunny nature of the Meseta, it's recommended to bring water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and comfortable walking shoes. Layers of clothing are useful for adapting to temperature changes, especially during spring and autumn. Snacks are also advisable for longer routes.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on the trails in Población De Campos?
A: Generally, the rural and open trails around Población De Campos are suitable for hiking with dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog under control, especially near agricultural fields or livestock, and to carry waste bags. Ensure your dog has access to water, particularly on sunny days.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Población De Campos?
A: The best times to hike in Población De Campos are spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable walking conditions. Summer can be very hot due to the open landscape, while winter can be cold.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Pablo Payo Pérez – View of the Meseta loop from Villarmentero de Campos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.3-mile (19.8 km) route. This trail offers more extensive exploration of the Meseta landscape.
tips: The bollards at the side of the road are unmistakably decorated with the shell symbol and show where to go.
Iron sculpture that commemorates the figure of the pilgrim who passes the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking trail?
faqs.answer: This circular trail is classified as a moderate hike. While it requires a good level of fitness, the paths are generally easily accessible, making it suitable for a range of hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily traverses the vast, open plains of Tierra de Campos, characterized by cultivated fields and mostly compact clay ground. However, the section along the Ucieza River offers a distinct change with a cooler and shadier environment.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail highlights its connection to the French Way of St. James. You can expect to see iconic pilgrimage markers. Specifically, you'll pass by the Camino de Santiago Marker in Población de Campos and the Pilgrim Statue in Revenga de Campos.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers sections of the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas) and the Camino Francés - 04 Burgos a León, which are part of the larger Camino de Santiago network. A small portion also follows the Camino del Río.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and easily accessible paths, the trail can be suitable for families with active children who are accustomed to hiking. The shady river section might be particularly pleasant for a family outing.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. The shady section along the Ucieza River can be particularly welcome during warmer periods in late spring or early summer.

faqs.question: Is the Symbols of the French Way of St. James in Tierra de Campos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not provided, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Spain, especially in rural areas. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Be mindful of agricultural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail. It is part of a public network of paths.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, the trail can likely be accessed from various points in the villages it passes through, such as Población de Campos or Revenga de Campos. Look for public parking areas within these villages to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through villages like Población de Campos and Revenga de Campos. These small towns along the Camino de Santiago typically offer basic amenities such as cafes, bars, and sometimes small shops or guesthouses where you can find refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, bring comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen) due to the open plains, and snacks. A light jacket might be useful for the shadier river sections or cooler weather. Don't forget your camera for the Camino markers!

Pilgrim Statue in Revenga de Campos
